High libertarianism and anti-censorship orientation support transparency, but low openness and high trust in institutions temper enthusiasm.

High openness to experience and strong moral concerns about liberty/anti-domination drive moderate support for algorithmic transparency, yet high trust in institutions and a traditional-authoritarian nationalist worldview temper enthusiasm for regulatory mandates.

High populist anti-elitism and distrust of institutions combine with moderate authoritarianism to support transparency as a check on powerful platforms, but very low openness limits enthusiasm for complex regulatory solutions.

High political efficacy and trust in institutions, combined with low openness and high economic leftism, drive moderate support for transparency as a tool for accountability, but low conscientiousness and libertarian skepticism of state control temper enthusiasm.
